title: malpighia coccigera subsp. horrida
intro: Malpighia coccigera subsp. horrida, also known as the wild guava or yellow mombin, is a sub-species of a tropical fruit-bearing tree native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malpighia coccigera subsp. horrida include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
